text: $SWC : Possible Path to 1000 Bitcoin within 100 days 1. $SWC first bought Bitcoin on April 28 and now holds 83.24 BTC but what would it take to hit 1,000 BTC by Day 100 (Aug 6)? 2. They need to acquire an additional 916.76 BTC in the next 68 days. 3. At a BTC price of $105,000, that’s $96.26M. Converted to GBP at 0.7432, that’s £71.51M. 4. They already have £6.998M in cash* , reducing the funding requirement to £64.52M. Spread evenly over the remaining 9.71 weeks, that’s £6.64M per week. 5. With a share price of 82.5p, this means issuing approximately 8.05 million shares per week. 6. Given 187.64M shares outstanding, that equates to ~4.29% dilution per week. 7. Over 9.71 weeks, if sustained, this would take them to 1,000 BTC . 8. If the share price was £1 and BTC $105,000, it would need to raise approximately £6.64m per week, issuing around 6.64 million shares weekly and causing ~3.44% dilution per week over the next 9.71 weeks to reach 1,000 BTC by August 6, assuming £6.998M in cash is used and the GBP/USD rate is 0.7432. 8. This scenario assumes BTC price remains flat and that all new capital is allocated to BTC. Execution risk, investor appetite, and market reaction will define what’s feasible. * cash number taken from @BitcoinBee21 thesmarterinvestmentguy.com/…

text: Summary from James’s session on $SWC - key takeaways from Vegas (all interpretations are my own) 1. Whilst there were a few who didn't know about $SWC , general awareness was significantly higher than expected. James noted that many people already know who $SWC are, understand what the company does, and are impressed by the pace of growth. 2. Multiple investors asked how to buy $SWC shares in the US. James explained that it’s currently possible via Fidelity. Once OTCQB trading goes live, access and liquidity for US investors will likely improve further. 3. There is growing curiosity around what’s next for $SWC . James described consistent conversations about the company’s trajectory and upcoming developments. 4. Andrew Webley’s work ethic came up several times - described as “next level.” He’s reportedly working late nights and early mornings to push the business forward. Reinforces confidence in the leadership team. 5. James and Andrew have recorded a new interview, which is scheduled to be released June 4. Expect more commentary on current strategy and vision. 6. James spoke with David Bailey and emphasised the need to “triple down on $SWC ,” signalling strong personal conviction and growing support from leaders in the Bitcoin space. 7. Some questions around the outstanding warrants issued at IPO. Some investors are concerned about dilution. According to the Admission Document (pages 9 and 14, link below), Andrew Webley has received warrants over approx. 25.8 million shares. These are exercisable one year after admission, up to the third anniversary, at the IPO placing price. James’s view is that while dilution is always a concern, the company’s growing equity base will reduce the relative impact of those warrants by the time they are exercisable. Moreover those who received the shares from the warrants are likely to be long term Hodlrs. 8. $SWC is now on Michael Saylor’s radar. When Andrew met Saylor, he was already aware of both $SWC and Andrew’s background. 9. Whilst still small within the Universe of Treasury Companies, $SWC currently has the lowest Days to Cover mNAV (42 days). Also has one of the lowest risk-adjusted months to cover (1.2) as per @RoaringRagnar 10. $SWC is now being included in more analyst comparisons with other Bitcoin treasury companies. This inclusion is building more visibility and institutional credibility. 11. $SWC is gaining momentum fast. More visibility, deeper conviction, and continued execution from the team. Useful references: Admission Document (warrant details): investors.smarterwebcompany.… Treasury metrics via @RoaringRagnar : x.com/RoaringRagnar/status/1…

text: see your point here, it’s a fair and important one. We need to be able to ask difficult questions early in the process. The challenge with the equity offering function is that it tends to work well during bull markets, when there’s strong natural demand. However, if we enter a period of consolidation or weakness, appetite is likely to diminish - unless the offering is priced at a discount, which, as Andrew has flagged he is not keen to do. We also need to consider how willing retail and institutional investors will be to keep paying up for stock (even in a bull market, eg I paid 16p last week, now I have to pay 27p etc). Retail are believers but institutionals can sometimes be more cautious. The ATM functionality, which is potentially achievable via an equity tap issuance in the UK (subject to certain conditions) would likely be most effective ONCE we see a meaningful increase in $SWC’s trading volume. For example, $MSTR uses a 3–7% TWAP of volume to issue passively, limiting price impact. Assuming a similar approach were possible for us, and with current average daily volume at around £1m, that equates to roughly £30-70k per day, or £150-350k per week—about 2 to 4.5 BTC weekly. If we can grow volume to £10m/day through increased awareness and a potential US listing, this rises to 20–45 BTC a week. Let me know your thoughts / feedback. Cheers

text: SUMMARY NOTES FROM EPISODE 2 * Participants: Andrew Webley and Tyler Evans * all views and interpretations are my own Recent equity raises have been successful. SWC appreciates the support (especially from retail). Whilst equity raises remain the preferred method of funding for now, they note they are operationally intensive and shouldn’t be expected on a weekly basis. SWC’s is having ongoing conversations with large institutions about becoming shareholders. SWC’s operational business continues to improve. Andrew is focused on reaching milestones within the list of the top 100 public Bitcoin holders and remains confident in SWC’s strategy and execution. OTCQB is considered the fastest route into the US market. The MTPLF ticker took months to gain traction, and building trading volume organically will require patience. Tyler confirmed that Michael Saylor is aware of SWC but shared no further details. SWC is currently UTXO’s core UK exposure. As UTXO’s strategy has matured, they’ve become more selective in their partnerships but continue to reserve flexibility. UTXO has doubled and tripled down on companies they’ve backed and expressed a desire to remain a long-term capital partner for SWC. This was reassuring to hear. Andrew welcomes competition among UK Bitcoin treasury companies. It doesn’t alter SWC’s approach, if anything, it increases their motivation. Tyler expects Bitcoin treasury companies to emerge in every region and urges investors to be discerning about which ones they support and understand the rationale behind their investments. Buybacks could be appropriate if SWC’s market NAV falls below 1 in a bearish market, but would need to be executed carefully (e.g. proportion of daily volume). It would be a mistake for a company to publicly commit to defending MNAV. Proof of reserves is expected at some point but not in the short term. SWC prefers to be judged on management’s actions to date. They currently use three custodians (possibly moving to four). Tyler acknowledged the growing power of community and recognized SWC’s efforts. This aligns with the trend of community-led investor relations, as seen with MSTR’s True North initiative. Research on SWC is now available on their website. Tennyson Securities has produced excellent, educational content, and SWC is grateful for their support. SWC was mentioned positively in the UK House of Parliament. Andrew stressed the importance of maintaining proper conduct and compliance with all regulations, while also showcasing the positive impact of a company putting Bitcoin on its balance sheet. Merchandise is coming soon- a prototype cap has been ordered with hopes of availability before Vegas. Pricing is expected to be £25 in the UK and £30 internationally. Some exciting updates around color selection are also in the works.

text: Hey, sure. When $SWC lists on the OTCQB, it’s not issuing any new shares. Second, it’s a cost effective way to tap into the US market. $SWC gets access to a wider pool of investors and more liquidity, without the time and expense of doing a full US IPO. text: Thanks for bringing much needed attention to this UK opportunity, $SWC . I wrote some thoughts earlier (see below) post another day of record volume for the company since IPO. Price action being driven by a combo of factors: - Continued momentum from last week - Hargreaves Lansdown (UK’s largest platform, ~40% market share) now offering access - Bullish Bernstein note on corporate BTC adoption - Retail exposure growing (see This is Money article, May 4) - Strong $SWC community sentiment Volume today already exceeds last week's total (partly expected given HL access) Seeing notable block trades too, likely early IPO holders flipping. I’m not surprised by the move. Set-up felt constructive. Momentum seems poised to continue as BTC buy news is likely staggered, retail FOMO builds, and liquidity remains thin. Expect volatility. With such a tight float, small capital moves price sharply. More IPO flips likely if we push higher, but also risk of sharp markdowns if large blocks hit the tape. On valuation: whilst MNAV is a key metric for valuing BTCs (Bitcoin Treasury Companies), MNAV may not be the right lens for now. Yes, it’s trading ~50x MNAV (based on 5.74 BTC + £20.6m mkt cap), but $SWC is a new-co with no comp in the UK. This isn't 21 Capital (coming with 41k BTC). $SWC is the first of its kind publicly in the UK, it's early-stage and UK retail has no BTC ETF option, with large scope for growth in BTC/sh. It's a different beast from $MSTR , $MTPLF , or $ALTBG who's MNAV's are driven by their own respective set of regional factors ex of their pure BTC holdings. Equity raise? Too early. Discovery phase just started - let price/volume settle. Remember: proceeds are just for BTC, but also to grow the core web biz. Dilution this early isn’t in everyone’s interest. Plus, who’s to say 50x is the cap? It’s arbitrary. With strong execution and narrative, over time it could be 60x, 100x... we’re in the discovery phase - we need data, patience and to manage expectations. Let's continue to engage constructively and positively. * NFA, all views my own.

text: Thanks for the feedback, James. Ultimately, this is where the corporate brokers step in - to start gathering feedback from both existing shareholders and potential new investors who might participate in a future raise. As @asjwebley mentioned on the Bitcoin Collective podcast, it’s about attracting the right kind of capital into $SWC . Apologies if you know this already but for the education of those that may not, most institutions have clear frameworks around IPO and follow-on participation: they’ll look at time since listing, the purpose of the raise, terms, daily liquidity (ADV), and broader sentiment. That’s why I think we need at least a month of trading data before seriously considering a raise. That doesn’t mean brokers and management can’t begin exploratory conversations now, but securing the best terms for $SWC likely requires more trading history. For example, many institutions are limited to investing only a % of average daily volume (ADV). If we hypothetically raised today with a £1m ADV, that significantly limits ticket size. Give it a month and if ADV grows to £10m, the pool of potential capital expands materially. Of course, timing and terms are everything and any raise would also depend on broader market conditions.

text: Thoughts on $SWC price action, the UKs first publicly traded BTC Holding Company. $SWC up another 2.8x (14p vs Friday 4.8p close), market cap £20.6m. Now up 5.6x from IPO price (2.5p). Price action being driven by a combo of factors: - Continued momentum from last week - Hargreaves Lansdown (UK’s largest platform, ~40% market share) now offering access - Bullish Bernstein note on corporate BTC adoption - Retail exposure growing (see This is Money article, May 4) - Strong $SWC community sentiment Volume today already exceeds last week's total (partly expected given HL access) Seeing notable block trades too, likely early IPO holders flipping. I’m not surprised by the move. Set-up felt constructive. Momentum seems poised to continue as BTC buy news is likely staggered, retail FOMO builds, and liquidity remains thin. Expect volatility. With such a tight float, small capital moves price sharply. More IPO flips likely if we push higher, but also risk of sharp markdowns if large blocks hit the tape. On valuation: whilst MNAV is a key metric for valuing BTCs (Bitcoin Treasury Companies), MNAV may not be the right lens for now. Yes, it’s trading ~50x MNAV (based on 5.74 BTC + £20.6m mkt cap), but $SWC is a new-co with no comp in the UK. This isn't 21 Capital (coming with 41k BTC). $SWC is the first of its kind publicly in the UK, it's early-stage and UK retail has no BTC ETF option, with large scope for growth in BTC/sh. It's a different beast from $MSTR , $MTPLF , or $ALTBG who's MNAV's are driven by their own respective set of regional factors ex of their pure BTC holdings. Equity raise? Too early. Discovery phase just started - let price/volume settle. Remember: proceeds are just for BTC, but also to grow the core web biz. Dilution this early isn’t in everyone’s interest. Plus, who’s to say 50x is the cap? It’s arbitrary. With strong execution and narrative, over time it could be 60x, 100x... we’re in the discovery phase - we need data, patience and to manage expectations. Let's continue to engage constructively and positively. * NFA, all views my own. @smarterwebuk @asjwebley
